scope.area: permission-policy fix validation across installer, setup, tenant scaffold, smoke, uninstall, and purge
coverage: root-group permission policy changes in runtime policy, setup, tenant creation, proxy setup, uninstall, and purge
disposable installer clone flow from a local bare snapshot
fresh `www.fakedomain.com` scaffold creation and on-disk permission inspection
loopback smoke validation with a strict 1 second curl timeout

summary.highLevelVerdict: The March 23, 2026 permission-policy follow-up run corrected the previously detected lifecycle blockers. The installer clone flow completed successfully, `www.fakedomain.com` was scaffolded with `root` group ownership on runtime and tenant paths, the manager booted without any `EACCES` loop, every local smoke route succeeded within the 1 second curl limit, uninstall removed `install-meta.env` correctly, and purge finished with no residual root PM2 `Ehecoatl` entry.

commands.snapshotPrep: Create `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23b-131335/source`, commit a disposable snapshot excluding `.git`, `.vscode`, `app/node_modules`, and `app/_log`, then clone it as `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23b-131335/remote.git`.
commands.loopbackSmoke: Use `curl -sk --max-time 1 -H 'Host: <host>' https://127.0.0.1<path>` for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session` with both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com`.

details: The lifecycle used a disposable source snapshot and local bare git remote, so the installer followed the true clone-to-`/opt/ehecoatl` path instead of reusing the authoring checkout.
The authoring checkout remained available for purge after uninstall removed `/opt/ehecoatl`.
Pre-run checks confirmed that `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, and the root PM2 app list were clean before the run.
The installer completed the true clone flow into `/opt/ehecoatl` and setup scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com` successfully.
The runtime and tenant paths were created with `root` as group instead of the previous `ehecoatl` or host-user group pattern.

Observed examples after setup included `/etc/opt/ehecoatl` as `ehecoatl:root 2775`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` as `ehecoatl:root 640`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/fakedomain.com/www` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 2755` with setgid, `config.json` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 640`, and writable tenant folders such as `cache`, `log`, and `spool` as `2775`.
`sudo npm run start` returned successfully in 487 ms and PM2 launched two root cluster instances immediately.
The first successful local smoke response arrived 45 ms after probing began, well within the 1 second curl timeout budget.

All checked routes passed with `--max-time 1` for both `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com`: `/` returned `302` to `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/index.htm` and `/htm/cached.htm` returned `200` HTML, `/hello` returned `200` JSON, and `/session` returned `200` JSON with `sessionId` and `sessionData`.
Observed route timings stayed below the 1 second cutoff, with the slowest successful route checks around 316 ms.
supportingObservations: PM2 logs showed both manager processes reach `ready` and later spawn `tenant_www.fakedomain.com` successfully.
A post-stop local curl returned exit code `7` with `HTTP_CODE=000`, which matches immediate connection refusal after shutdown.
A grep check found no `EACCES`, `permission denied`, or manager crash-loop messages in the live log tree before purge.

details: The uninstall script removed `/opt/ehecoatl` and `/usr/local/bin/ehecoatl` successfully.
The expected preserved data trees under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remained after uninstall.
Unlike the previous failing runs, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` was already absent immediately after uninstall.
After purge, `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` were all absent.
The root PM2 process list was empty after purge, so no manual `pm2 delete` follow-up was required.

requiredChecks.item: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
requiredChecks.note: A disposable snapshot, bare remote, and separate authoring checkout were used.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime-policy defaults reflect the new `root` group ownership model consistently
requiredChecks.note: Observed runtime and tenant paths used the `root` group, and runtime users `ehecoatl`, `e_manager`, and `e_engine` had primary gid `root`.

requiredChecks.item: A fresh `www.fakedomain.com` scaffold no longer reproduces manager `EACCES` for host `config.json`
requiredChecks.note: The manager reached `ready`, tenant processes spawned successfully, and log checks found no matching permission errors.

requiredChecks.item: Loopback access proves `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` with a 1 second curl timeout
requiredChecks.note: All tested routes succeeded within the `--max-time 1` limit.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
requiredChecks.note: A direct stop completed in 1894 ms and left the PM2 app in stopped state before uninstall.

requiredChecks.item: Uninstall removes `install-meta.env` correctly while preserving runtime data
requiredChecks.note: `install-meta.env` was absent immediately after uninstall and the runtime data trees remained until purge.

requiredChecks.item: Purge removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` trees and leaves no PM2 residue
requiredChecks.note: All preserved trees were removed and the root PM2 list was empty after purge.

requiredChecks.item: A new performance report records whether the previously detected problems were corrected
requiredChecks.note: This report is the matching follow-up artifact.

correctedProblems: The manager no longer crash-loops on `EACCES` when opening `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/<domain>/www/config.json`.
Local smoke checks now succeed instead of returning `HTTP_CODE=000`.
Purge now leaves no stopped root PM2 `Ehecoatl` entry.
remainingCaveats: The chosen implementation resolves the earlier write-policy ambiguity by keeping regular files owner-writable while using `root` group access plus setgid directories for shared traversal and selected writable folders.

nextAction.validatedRevisionOutcome: The permission-policy review tasklist produced a passing installer-loopback-uninstall lifecycle on March 23, 2026.
recommendedFollowUp: If this permission contract is the intended long-term model, document it in setup and tenancy reference docs so operators understand the `root` group and setgid directory behavior.
If broader policy hardening is still desired, add direct regression tests or shell-based verification scripts for ownership, group, and mode expectations after setup and `tenant_create`.
